title: Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026: Fully Paid Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in Nairobi, Kenya
summary: Young people across Kenya now have an exciting opportunity to become part of a growing movement focused on climate action, sustainability, and community transformation through the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026. The program is designed to empower young leaders with climate knowledge, digital literacy, leadership abilities, and practical community engagement skills needed to drive meaningful environmental change. The Climate Champions initiative by Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya (DOT Kenya) is not just another training opportunity. It is a youth-led climate action movement that seeks to mobilize young people to become active changemakers in their communities. Through training, mentorship, participatory research, and climate innovation, selected participants will work directly with communities to co-create and implement local climate solutions. This opportunity comes at a critical time when climate change continues to affect communities across Kenya and beyond. By investing in youth leadership and grassroots innovation, DOT Kenya aims to strengthen community resilience while creating pathways for young people to gain practical experience, leadership exposure, and career-enhancing skills. The application deadline is May 26, 2026 . About Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya is a locally registered non-governmental organization established in 2005 and officially registered in 2007. The organization focuses on empowering underserved and disadvantaged youth with: Digital literacy 21st-century skills Leadership development Innovation and entrepreneurship skills Confidence to thrive in the digital economy DOT Kenya operates as part of the broader DOT Global network, which works across Sub-Saharan Africa, the Middle East, and the United Kingdom. Over the years, the organization has supported thousands of young people in building sustainable livelihoods while becoming leaders in their communities. What is the Digital Opportunity Trust Climate Program? The DOT Climate Program is a youth-centered initiative focused on building a movement of climate-conscious young leaders capable of driving change at the grassroots level. The program is based on the belief that young people are powerful agents of change who can: Influence positive behavior change Mobilize communities toward sustainability Design local climate solutions Promote climate awareness Lead environmental action projects Through the program, selected Climate Champions will receive specialized training and tools that will help them engage communities in climate action and environmental sustainability initiatives. Overview of the Cisco Climate Project The Cisco-supported Climate Champions initiative seeks to scale youth-led climate action across communities in Kenya and Jordan. Through funding support from Cisco, DOT Kenya will recruit and train digitally skilled youth leaders who will serve as Climate Champions in their local communities. The project will equip participants with: DOT Climate Curriculum Participatory Action Research (PAR) Toolkit Community mobilization skills Digital advocacy skills Climate coaching techniques Leadership and communication skills After training, Climate Champions will work directly with communities to identify environmental challenges and co-design practical climate solutions. The project emphasizes local ownership, sustainability, and community participation in addressing climate-related issues. Role of the Climate Champions Selected participants will work closely with DOT Kenya to deliver environmental education and climate awareness initiatives in Nairobi and surrounding areas. As a Climate Champion, participants will help communities: Understand climate change challenges Build resilience Develop sustainable environmental practices Implement community-driven climate solutions This is a short-term but highly impactful leadership opportunity for youth interested in environmental sustainability, climate advocacy, digital innovation, and community development. Program Details Position Title Climate Champion Program Duration 3 Months Location Nairobi, Kenya Reporting To Program Coordinator Training Structure Selected Climate Champions will participate in a 4-week hybrid training program beginning in June 2026. The training will include: Three weeks of virtual learning sessions One week of physical in-person training Participants will receive hands-on learning experiences focused on climate action, participatory research, community engagement, and leadership development. Key Responsibilities of Climate Champions Successful applicants will undertake several important responsibilities during the program. These responsibilities include: 1. Conducting Community Surveys Participants will conduct baseline surveys to better understand local environmental and climate-related challenges within communities. 2. Mobilizing Community Members Climate Champions will engage and coach community members on sustainable environmental practices and climate resilience strategies. 3. Leading Participatory Action Research Groups Participants will form and lead Participatory Action Research (PAR) working groups consisting of 7–9 members. 4. Gathering Community Insights Champions will use participatory methods to gather community perspectives and identify local climate priorities. 5. Co-Designing Climate Solutions Participants will work collaboratively with communities to develop and implement locally driven climate action solutions. 6. Presenting Outcomes Climate Champions will present project outcomes, lessons learned, and climate solutions developed during the program. 7. Reporting and Continuous Learning Participants will submit reports and participate in continuous improvement and learning activities throughout the program period. 8. Global Collaboration Champions will engage with climate leaders and organizations globally through virtual discussions, networking opportunities, and collaborative sessions. 9. Digital Advocacy Participants will strengthen their social media and digital advocacy skills to promote climate awareness and sustainable solutions online. Benefits of the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program The program offers numerous benefits for selected participants. Monthly Stipends Climate Champions will receive monthly stipends during the program period. Certificate of Completion Participants who successfully complete the program will receive certificates recognizing their participation and achievements. Access to a Global Climate Network Selected youth will gain access to international climate leaders, networks, and collaborations. DOT Alumni Membership Graduates of the program will become members of the DOT Alumni network, opening doors to future opportunities and collaborations. Career Advancement Opportunities Participants will gain practical climate action project experience that can strengthen their professional portfolios and career prospects. Development of 21st-Century Skills The program will help participants strengthen: Communication skills Leadership skills Negotiation abilities Team collaboration skills Critical thinking skills Digital Literacy Skills Climate Champions will improve their knowledge and use of: Social media platforms Digital communication tools Research methodologies Data collection and analysis Climate Coaching Skills Participants will gain practical climate coaching skills, including the use of indigenous knowledge systems in climate adaptation and sustainability practices. National and Regional Visibility Outstanding Climate Champions may receive opportunities to showcase their work at regional and national events. Important Eligibility Information DOT Kenya has clarified that this opportunity is strictly open to new applicants only. Young people who have previously participated in or benefited from earlier DOT Kenya Cisco Climate Champions cohorts or projects are not eligible to apply. The organization is prioritizing youth who have not previously had access to Cisco-supported climate programming. Why This Opportunity Matters Climate change remains one of the most pressing global challenges affecting livelihoods, food security, public health, and environmental sustainability. Young people are increasingly becoming central to climate advocacy and innovation worldwide. The DOT Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program recognizes the importance of empowering local youth leaders who understand the realities within their communities and can develop sustainable, locally relevant solutions. Participants will not only gain valuable technical and leadership experience but also contribute directly to environmental sustainability and community resilience efforts in Kenya. Application Deadline Applications for the Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026 close on May 26, 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to apply early because interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis during the application period, and positions may be filled before the official deadline. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here . The post Digital Opportunity Trust Kenya Cisco Climate Champions Program 2026: Fully Paid Youth Climate Leadership Opportunity in Nairobi, Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Multilingual AI for Health Challenge 2026: Build AI Systems for African Languages and Win Up to $5,000 USD
summary: Access to reliable healthcare information remains one of the biggest challenges across many African communities, particularly in underserved and low-resource settings where language barriers limit access to accurate medical guidance. To address this gap, the Multilingual Health Question Answering in Low-Resource African Languages Challenge has officially launched on Zindi, inviting AI innovators, developers, researchers, machine learning engineers, and language technology enthusiasts to build multilingual AI systems capable of answering health-related questions in African languages. The challenge is organized in collaboration with the Hub for Artificial Intelligence in Maternal, Sexual and Reproductive Health (HASH) and focuses specifically on maternal, sexual, and reproductive health information in African communities. Participants will work on creating AI models that can understand and generate accurate, fluent, and culturally appropriate responses in languages such as Akan, Kiswahili, Luganda, and Amharic. The competition offers a total prize pool of $5,000 USD and is fully online, making it accessible to participants from across the globe. Why This Challenge Matters Despite the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence and natural language processing technologies, most large language models are still heavily trained on English and other high-resource languages. As a result, millions of African language speakers remain underserved by AI-powered healthcare systems. This challenge seeks to change that reality by encouraging the development of multilingual health assistants capable of supporting African communities in their native languages. Such systems could eventually power: Rural health worker assistants Community healthcare support systems Maternal health education tools Adolescent sexual health platforms HIV and STI awareness systems Clinic support applications Patient education chatbots By enabling people to ask sensitive health questions privately and receive responses in their own language, these AI systems could significantly improve healthcare accessibility, awareness, and informed decision-making across Africa. About the Organizers The challenge is spearheaded by the Hub for Artificial Intelligence in Maternal , Sexual and Reproductive Health ( HASH ), a multidisciplinary consortium bringing together experts in healthcare, public health, artificial intelligence, data science, and social sciences. The consortium includes: Infectious Disease Institute (IDI) Makerere University Makerere Centre for Artificial Intelligence (MAK-AI) Sunbird AI HASH focuses on advancing AI-driven healthcare solutions across Sub-Saharan Africa, particularly in areas such as: Maternal health HIV care Sexually transmitted infections Adolescent sexual and reproductive health Their goal is to promote locally grounded AI innovations tailored specifically to African realities and healthcare systems. Challenge Objective Participants are required to build a multilingual AI model capable of: Understanding health-related questions posed in low-resource African languages Generating accurate and contextually appropriate answers Responding fluently in the same language as the question The challenge dataset consists of curated health question-and-answer pairs focused on maternal, sexual, and reproductive health. Competitors are encouraged to use open-source tools, publicly available pretrained models, and freely accessible datasets where applicable. Supported Skills and Technologies This challenge is especially suitable for individuals interested in: Large Language Models (LLMs) Natural Language Processing (NLP) Machine Translation Multilingual AI Low-resource language modeling Deep learning Healthcare AI systems Participants will gain hands-on experience building socially impactful AI technologies for African communities. Prize Pool The challenge offers the following prizes: 1st Prize — $2,500 USD 2nd Prize — $1,500 USD 3rd Prize — $1,000 USD Additionally, participants can earn: 5,000 Zindi points Recognition within the African AI ecosystem Opportunities for collaboration and visibility in AI for healthcare Evaluation Metrics Submissions will be evaluated using a multi-metric scoring system combining lexical overlap, structural similarity, and semantic judgment. ROUGE-1 F1 (37%) Measures overlap of individual words between prediction and reference answers. ROUGE-L F1 (37%) Measures sentence structure similarity using the Longest Common Subsequence (LCS). LLM-as-a-Judge (26%) Uses a language model to evaluate: Factual accuracy Completeness Language appropriateness Overall response quality Top-performing solutions will also undergo additional evaluation using AfroLM BertScore F1, which measures semantic similarity using embeddings from AfroLM : a multilingual transformer pretrained on 23 African languages. Important Rules and Requirements Participants should note the following: Maximum team size: 4 people Fully online participation Open-source tools only No paid services or proprietary tools allowed Automated machine learning tools are prohibited Maximum of 5 submissions per day Maximum of 50 submissions overall Top 10 teams must submit reproducible code and reports Final winners must publicly share their code and models The challenge also enforces strict anti-cheating policies, including penalties and disqualification for multiple accounts, unauthorized collaboration, or unreproducible submissions. Webinar Information Participants are encouraged to register for the official webinar scheduled for: Date: 20 May 2026 Time: 6:00 PM – 7:00 PM GMT+2 The webinar will likely provide guidance on: Dataset structure Evaluation methods Submission process Baseline models Best practices for multilingual healthcare AI systems Deadline Application Deadline: 21 July 2026 Why You Should Participate This challenge is more than a competition. It is an opportunity to contribute to the future of inclusive healthcare technology in Africa. By participating, you can: Build real-world AI solutions with social impact Strengthen your NLP and LLM expertise Gain exposure within the African AI ecosystem Collaborate with innovators across the continent Contribute to healthcare accessibility in underserved communities Compete for international recognition and cash prizes For students, researchers, startups, healthcare innovators, and machine learning engineers passionate about African language technologies, this challenge represents a rare opportunity to combine technical innovation with meaningful impact. Deadline 21st July ,2026. APPLY HERE For more information on this opportunity, click here. For more global opportunities, click here. title: Fully Funded EU Delegation Traineeships 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland for Young Graduates | Apply for International Relations, Human Rights, Health, Labour & Diplomacy Opportunities
summary: The European Union Delegation to the United Nations and other international organisations in Geneva has officially opened applications for its fully funded traineeship programme for young graduates in 2026. This highly competitive opportunity is designed for emerging professionals interested in diplomacy, multilateral affairs, international relations, development cooperation, human rights, health governance, labour policy, economic affairs, and disarmament. Hosted in Geneva, Switzerland, the traineeships provide selected candidates with the opportunity to work directly within the EU Delegation and gain first-hand experience in the operations of one of the world’s most influential diplomatic missions. Participants will contribute to the European Union’s engagement with United Nations agencies and international organisations while strengthening their analytical, policy, research, and diplomatic skills. The programme offers traineeships ranging from three to six months, with tentative start dates in September or October 2026, depending on the section. The Disarmament and Non-Proliferation traineeship is scheduled to begin in November 2026. About the EU Delegation Traineeship Programme The traineeship programme aims to support young graduates who are passionate about international cooperation and multilateral diplomacy. Through this initiative, participants will gain direct exposure to the workings of the European Union in Geneva and understand how the EU represents its interests and values within global institutions. Geneva is one of the world’s most important diplomatic hubs, hosting major United Nations agencies and international organisations. As a trainee, participants will have the chance to engage in policy discussions, attend high-level meetings, support negotiations, conduct research, prepare reports, and assist in official diplomatic activities. The programme is especially valuable for graduates planning careers in: International diplomacy Human rights advocacy Development cooperation Labour and social affairs Health policy Economic affairs Climate and environmental governance Digital governance International security and disarmament European Union institutions United Nations agencies Available Traineeship Sections Applicants may apply to only one traineeship section. However, based on a candidate’s profile, the Delegation may recommend placement in another section. 1. Human Rights Section The Human Rights Section traineeship focuses on supporting EU engagement with international human rights mechanisms and processes. Main Responsibilities Selected trainees may: Contribute to drafting briefings Assist in meeting preparations Conduct policy research Draft statements Prepare reports on EU and UN human rights meetings Analyse human rights developments globally Support logistics and coordination Participate in Human Rights Council meetings Attend Universal Periodic Review sessions and working groups This traineeship is ideal for candidates interested in global human rights governance and international law. 2. Economic Affairs, Development, Environment and Digital Section This section focuses on the EU’s engagement with international organisations dealing with economics, development, climate, technology, and digital governance. Main Responsibilities Trainees may: Support the Head of Section and policy officers Assist in work related to UNECE, ITU, UNCTAD, WIPO, UNEP, WMO, and other organisations Prepare research and policy statements Draft reports after conferences and meetings Summarize newly issued international reports Support official visits and logistics This section is suitable for candidates interested in sustainable development, climate diplomacy, economic policy, innovation, and digital governance. 3. Social Affairs and Labour Section This traineeship focuses heavily on cooperation with the International Labour Organization (ILO) and related social policy areas. Main Responsibilities Participants may: Prepare analytical reports and briefing notes Summarize ILO meetings and seminars Support EU coordination activities Research labour and social policy issues Draft reports and policy analyses Attend meetings organized by the UN, NGOs, academia, and EU institutions Assist in International Labour Conference preparations This section is particularly beneficial for graduates interested in labour rights, employment policy, social justice, and international labour standards. 4. Health Section The Health Section traineeship offers opportunities to work on global public health and WHO-related activities. Main Responsibilities Selected trainees may: Assist colleagues covering EU activities at WHO Attend international health meetings and events Prepare analytical reports and background notes Support drafting of policy documents Participate in WHO meetings and coordination activities Conduct research on global health topics This traineeship is ideal for graduates in public health, health governance, medicine, policy studies, and international development. 5. Head of Delegation Section The Head of Delegation section provides exposure to high-level diplomatic coordination and cross-policy issues. Main Responsibilities Trainees may: Assist on crisis response and migration issues Participate in diplomatic meetings Prepare briefing materials Support event and mission organization Assist visiting EU Headquarters delegations This traineeship is ideal for candidates interested in diplomacy, international crisis response, migration policy, and global governance. 6. Disarmament and Non-Proliferation Section This traineeship is scheduled to begin in November 2026 and focuses on international security and arms control issues. Main Responsibilities Participants may: Support work related to UN disarmament meetings Assist in preparing international conferences Draft reports and meeting summaries Support official visits Provide logistical assistance This section is highly relevant for candidates pursuing careers in international security, peacebuilding, and non-proliferation policy. Duration of the Traineeships The traineeships will last between: 3 months 6 months Tentative start dates are: September 2026 October 2026 The Disarmament traineeship is expected to start in November 2026. Financial Benefits Selected trainees will receive a monthly grant intended to support living expenses during the traineeship period. Financial Support Includes Monthly stipend Professional international work experience Exposure to EU and UN diplomacy Networking opportunities Participation in global policy discussions Career development opportunities Costs Not Covered Applicants should note that certain expenses remain their responsibility, including: Travel expenses Visa costs Insurance Accommodation Relocation costs Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements. Academic Qualifications Candidates should possess: A Master’s degree (MA) in relevant fields such as: International Relations European Studies Human Rights Public Health International Development International Law Labour Studies Political Science Security Studies Other related disciplines Skills and Competencies Applicants should demonstrate: Strong knowledge of the European Union and the United Nations Excellent drafting and communication skills in English Knowledge of French is considered a strong advantage Analytical and research skills Interest in diplomacy and international policy Additional Assets The following are considered advantageous: Previous internships Volunteer experience Student leadership Policy research experience Experience related to the chosen section Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Europass Curriculum Vitae (CV) Cover letter Completed application form Only Europass-format CVs will be accepted. Important Application Instructions Candidates must clearly mention the following in the subject line of their email: “Funded Traineeship Name/Acronym of the Section” Applications submitted in incorrect formats may not be considered. Visa and Legal Requirements Selected trainees must comply with local legal requirements. EU citizens may need to: Show proof of health and pandemic insurance Obtain relevant visas or residence permits where required Why This Opportunity Is Important This traineeship offers exceptional exposure to: International diplomacy EU policymaking Multilateral negotiations United Nations systems Global governance International cooperation It is an ideal stepping stone for graduates seeking future careers in: EU institutions United Nations agencies NGOs International organizations Think tanks Public policy Government ministries Application Deadline The deadline for applications is: May 31, 2026 How to Apply Applications must be submitted by email to the section-specific addresses listed below. Candidates may only apply to one section per delegation cycle. Section-Specific Email Addresses: Human Rights Section: delegation-geneva-human-rights-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Economic Affairs Section: delegation-geneva-economic-affairs-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Social Affairs and Labour Section: delegation-geneva-social-affairs-and-labour-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Health Section: delegation-geneva-health-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Head of Delegation Section: delegation-geneva-head-of-delegation-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Disarmament Section: delegation-geneva-disarmament-traineeship@eeas.europa.eu Required Documents: A detailed, standard Europass Curriculum Vitae (CV) — CVs in any other format will not be considered. Create yours at: https://europa.eu/europass/en A cover letter explaining why you want to participate in an EU traineeship and why you are suited to the specific section you are applying to A completed application form (as specified by the delegation) Email Subject Line Format: Funded Traineeship [Name/Acronym of the Section] Before applying you are requested to examine the dedicated website as well as the general eligibility criteria for a paid traineeship (Article 8 of the Decision ADMIN(2017)28 – Paid traineeship). Selection Process After the evaluation of all applications: Shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview Unsuccessful candidates will be notified by email If selected candidates are unable to present the required documents upon request, their application will be rejected Applications from non-eligible candidates will not be considered under any circumstances Application Deadline 31 May 2026 — This is a firm deadline. Late or incomplete applications will not be accepted. Why Apply? This traineeship represents an extraordinary opportunity to: Gain direct, hands-on experience working within one of the world s most important diplomatic missions Understand how the European Union operates within the multilateral UN system Build professional networks with EU diplomats, UN officials, and representatives from member states and civil society Develop advanced skills in policy analysis, diplomatic reporting, and international negotiations Strengthen your candidacy for future roles in EU institutions, international organisations, NGOs, or national foreign ministries Experience life and work in Geneva, Switzerland — a global hub for diplomacy and international governance Click HERE to read more about the EU Delegation Traineeships 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland. Final Thoughts The Funded EU Delegation Traineeships in Geneva 2026 present an outstanding opportunity for young graduates passionate about diplomacy, international affairs, development, human rights, labour policy, health governance, and multilateral cooperation. By working directly within the EU Delegation to the United Nations and international organisations in Geneva, participants will gain valuable professional experience in one of the world’s most important diplomatic environments. For graduates looking to launch careers in international policy and global governance, this traineeship programme offers a prestigious and career-defining opportunity. Discover more global Internship , European , and UN opportunities on OFY. title: University of Melbourne Human Rights Scholarship 2026 in Australia | Fully Funded Graduate Research Scholarship for International Students
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Human Rights Scholarship 2026 at the University of Melbourne in Australia. This highly competitive fully funded scholarship supports domestic and international students who wish to undertake graduate research focused on human rights and social justice. The scholarship is designed for outstanding students committed to the peaceful advancement of human rights through academic research, advocacy, professional work, or voluntary engagement. Successful applicants will receive comprehensive financial support including full tuition coverage, a generous living stipend, relocation assistance, and Overseas Student Health Cover for eligible international students. Hosted by Australia’s top-ranked university, this scholarship provides exceptional opportunities for future researchers, policymakers, advocates, and global leaders seeking to create meaningful impact in the field of human rights. About the University of Melbourne The University of Melbourne is one of the world’s leading universities and is consistently ranked as Australia’s number one university. Located in Melbourne, Victoria, the institution is globally recognized for: Research excellence Academic innovation International collaborations Graduate employability Contributions to social impact and policy development The university offers world-class graduate research opportunities across numerous disciplines and provides students access to internationally respected academics, cutting-edge research facilities, and interdisciplinary learning environments. Official Website: The University of Melbourne Overview of the Human Rights Scholarship 2026 The Human Rights Scholarship is specifically intended for graduate researchers whose academic work contributes to advancing understanding, protection, and promotion of human rights. The scholarship supports research projects that address: Human dignity Social justice Equality International human rights law Public policy Peacebuilding Humanitarian issues Advocacy and justice systems The scholarship recognizes students who demonstrate commitment to human rights not only academically but also through: Volunteer activities Advocacy work Professional experience Community engagement Scholarship Details Scholarship Name Human Rights Scholarship 2026 Institution University of Melbourne Study Location Melbourne, Australia Study Level Graduate Research Eligible Degree Programmes Master by Research Doctoral Research Degree (PhD) Eligible Applicants Domestic and International Students Number of Scholarships 4 scholarships available Application Timeline Applications Open 1 April 2026 Application Deadline 31 October 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all supporting documents are completed before the deadline. Scholarship Benefits The University of Melbourne Human Rights Scholarship offers one of the most comprehensive graduate research funding packages in Australia. 1. Full Tuition Fee Remission Recipients will receive: Full fee offset for up to 2 years for Master by Research students Full fee offset for up to 4 years for doctoral students This ensures students can focus fully on their research without tuition-related financial stress. 2. Generous Living Allowance Successful applicants will receive: AUD $44,500 per year (2026 full-time study rate) The stipend is: Paid pro rata Indexed annually Designed to support living expenses during study Duration of Living Allowance Up to 2 years for Master by Research students Up to 3.5 years for Doctoral students The allowance also includes: Limited paid sick leave Maternity leave Parenting leave 3. Relocation Grant Students relocating to Melbourne may receive: AUD $2,000 for students moving from outside Victoria AUD $3,000 for students relocating from outside Australia This support helps cover travel and settlement expenses. 4. Overseas Student Health Cover (OSHC) International students who require a student visa will receive: Overseas Student Health Cover (Single Membership) This helps international students access healthcare services while studying in Australia. Total Scholarship Value The estimated scholarship value ranges between: AUD $89,000 – AUD $155,000 The final amount depends on: Degree level Duration of study Individual circumstances Duration of the Scholarship The scholarship duration varies depending on the selected research programme. Master by Research Students Up to 2 years Doctoral Students Up to 3.5 years for living allowance Up to 4 years for tuition coverage Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the Human Rights Scholarship, applicants must: Have received an offer for a graduate research degree commencement in the scholarship year Intend to undertake graduate research in the field of Human Rights Not have already completed a research qualification at the same or higher level as the intended programme Demonstrate strong commitment to the advancement of human rights The university places significant emphasis on applicants who combine academic excellence with practical engagement in human rights issues. Selection Criteria Selection is highly competitive and based on: Academic merit Course entry score Quality of the scholarship application Demonstrated commitment to human rights Potential impact of the proposed research The scholarship is awarded to applicants ranked highest by the selection panel. Human Rights Commitment Requirement Applicants must clearly explain: Their commitment to the peaceful advancement of respect for human rights Relevant volunteer or professional experiences How their proposed research contributes to human rights understanding Their future contribution to the human rights field This requirement is a central component of the application process. Why This Scholarship is Important The Human Rights Scholarship provides students with the opportunity to: Conduct impactful human rights research Study at Australia’s leading university Access world-class academic mentorship Build international networks Contribute to social justice and policy development Advance global understanding of human rights issues The programme is particularly valuable for aspiring: Researchers Human rights advocates Policy professionals Academics Lawyers NGO leaders International development specialists Graduate Research Opportunities at Melbourne Graduate researchers at the University of Melbourne benefit from: Interdisciplinary research support Advanced research infrastructure Access to global academic networks Collaboration opportunities Professional development programmes Students are encouraged to engage with broader academic and policy communities during their research journey. How to Apply Applicants must submit: An online application A statement addressing their commitment to human rights Information about volunteer or professional experience Explanation of how their research contributes to the human rights field Official Scholarship Page University of Melbourne Human Rights Scholarship 2026 Additional Resources Graduate Research Scholarships Information When Will Applicants Receive Results? Applicants will be informed of the scholarship outcome by: February of the following year. The university has stated that: Individual feedback will not be provided Reasons for unsuccessful applications will not be disclosed Important Notes for Applicants Applicants should note: Scholarship information may change without notice Competition is extremely high Strong research proposals significantly improve chances of selection Human rights engagement outside academia is highly valued Applicants are encouraged to carefully prepare a compelling application demonstrating both academic excellence and social impact. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Scholarship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post University of Melbourne Human Rights Scholarship 2026 in Australia | Fully Funded Graduate Research Scholarship for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF Youth Consultant Opportunity 2026 in Italy: Home-Based Paid Consultancy for Young Leaders Supporting U-Report and Youth Engagement
summary: The UNICEF has officially announced applications for the 2026 Youth Consultant Opportunity under the UNICEF ECARO Italy Office, offering young people in Italy the chance to contribute to one of UNICEF’s flagship youth engagement platforms while gaining practical experience in international development, youth participation, digital engagement, and social impact initiatives. This consultancy opportunity is part of UNICEF’s broader mission to empower young people and amplify youth voices through U-Report, a global digital platform that enables youth participation in conversations around education, mental health, climate change, protection, and social issues affecting young people worldwide. The consultancy is home-based in Italy and runs from May 2026 to December 2026, providing selected youth consultants with opportunities to engage directly with UNICEF programme teams, contribute to digital campaigns, co-design youth polls, and support initiatives that foster positive social change. About UNICEF and U-Report UNICEF operates in more than 190 countries and territories, working to protect the rights and wellbeing of children and young people around the world. One of UNICEF’s most innovative youth engagement initiatives is: U-Report U-Report is a digital engagement platform designed to: Empower youth voices Promote civic participation Gather youth perspectives through polls Provide lifesaving and protective information Encourage youth-led development Create positive social change The platform allows young people to express their opinions on issues affecting their lives while connecting them to relevant information and opportunities. Topics addressed through U-Report include: Mental health Education Climate change Youth empowerment Protection and safety Inclusion and discrimination Social wellbeing About the UNICEF Youth Consultant Opportunity UNICEF ECARO Italy is recruiting two additional youth consultants to join the U-Report Steering Group for a nine-month consultancy assignment. The steering group helps guide: Youth engagement strategies Poll development Youth outreach Digital participation activities Programme recommendations Selected consultants will support UNICEF’s efforts to ensure youth perspectives remain central to programme development and implementation. Consultancy Duration The consultancy period runs: From May 2026 to December 2026 The assignment covers: 26.6 working days across 8 months Work Location This consultancy is: Home-based in Italy Selected consultants are expected to work remotely while participating in: Online meetings Collaborative sessions Programme activities Occasional in-person events within Italy UNICEF Programme Focus Areas The U-Report Steering Group is divided into five sub-groups aligned with UNICEF ECARO programme priorities: 1. Protection Focus on: Child protection Youth safety Rights awareness Social protection initiatives 2. Alternative Care Addressing: Child welfare Family support systems Youth care structures 3. Intersectional Discrimination Working on: Equity and inclusion Anti-discrimination advocacy Marginalized youth issues 4. Quality Education Focused on: Skills development Job orientation Youth education opportunities Learning access 5. Mental Health and Psychosocial Wellbeing Addressing: Youth mental health Emotional wellbeing Psychosocial support Community resilience Responsibilities of Youth Consultants Selected consultants will contribute to several youth engagement and programme activities. 1. Participate in Steering Group Meetings Consultants will: Attend monthly meetings Contribute to discussions Support programme planning Collaborate with fellow youth representatives Meetings may be: Online Face-to-face 2. Collaborate with UNICEF Programme Teams Youth consultants will engage with programme teams focused on: Protection Mental health Education Youth wellbeing Responsibilities include: Providing feedback Supporting programme development Sharing youth perspectives 3. Co-Design U-Report Polls Consultants will: Suggest relevant youth topics Draft poll questions Help shape engagement campaigns 4. Analyze Poll Results Participants will: Review survey responses Discuss findings Contribute recommendations for programme improvements 5. Support Digital Content Creation Youth consultants may contribute to: Instagram campaigns TikTok content Facebook outreach Youth-focused digital communication 6. Promote U-Report Consultants are expected to: Engage youth networks Expand participation Connect with community groups Increase awareness of U-Report initiatives 7. Participate in Events and Forums Selected participants may: Attend youth forums Represent youth perspectives Engage in public discussions Support advocacy activities Benefits of the Consultancy Opportunity This opportunity provides valuable exposure and professional development for young people interested in youth advocacy and international development. 1. Experience Working with UNICEF Participants gain practical experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations. 2. Professional Development Youth consultants strengthen: Communication skills Leadership skills Digital engagement expertise Team collaboration Programme development knowledge 3. Networking Opportunities Participants engage with: UNICEF programme teams Youth leaders Community organizations International development professionals 4. Youth Advocacy Experience The consultancy allows participants to: Influence youth-focused initiatives Promote social change Amplify youth perspectives 5. Travel Opportunities Within Italy Consultants may participate in approved travel activities related to programme implementation. UNICEF will reimburse eligible travel expenses up to: EUR 250 Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all mandatory requirements. Age Requirement Applicants must be: Between 18–24 years old Residency Requirement Applicants must: Reside in Italy Education Requirement Applicants must possess: A secondary school certificate Language Requirement Applicants must demonstrate: Fluency in Italian (written and spoken) Technical Requirement Applicants must: Own a mobile phone Have reliable internet access for video calls Skills Requirement Applicants should demonstrate: Strong interpersonal skills Teamwork abilities Motivation and initiative Contextual Understanding Applicants should understand: Community issues Youth-related challenges Issues affecting youth with migratory backgrounds Desirable Qualifications Additional qualifications considered valuable include: Community volunteering experience Grassroots engagement experience Knowledge of youth advocacy Additional language skills Languages considered an advantage include: English French Dari Pashtu Wolof UNICEF’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion UNICEF emphasizes: Diversity Inclusion Equal opportunity Child safeguarding Anti-discrimination policies The organization encourages applications from individuals regardless of: Race Ethnicity Gender identity Sexual orientation Disability status Socio-economic background Travel and Security Requirements Consultants traveling within Italy must: Obtain prior UNICEF approval Follow UN security procedures Maintain valid travel and medical insurance Comply with local security guidance Why This Opportunity Matters This consultancy provides young people with: International organizational experience Youth participation leadership opportunities Digital engagement exposure Advocacy and policy experience Professional development within the UN system The programme demonstrates UNICEF’s commitment to ensuring young people actively participate in shaping the policies and programmes that affect their lives. Important Notes This consultancy is: A re-advertisement with minor amendments Previous applicants do not need to reapply Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Consultants are responsible for: Personal tax obligations Maintaining valid health insurance Ensuring visa compliance where applicable Application Deadline The official deadline to apply for the UNICEF Youth Consultant Opportunity 2026 is: 17 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit applications early due to the competitive nature of UNICEF opportunities. Interested applicants should apply through the Official UNICEF Careers Portal before the 17 May 2026 deadline. Click HERE to learn more about the UNICEF Youth Consultant Opportunity 2026 in Italy. Discover more global Conference and UN opportunities on OFY. title: IEJ Senior Researcher: Global Economic Governance Job in South Africa 2026 | Fully Detailed Guide on Salary, Eligibility, Responsibilities, and How to Apply
summary: The Institute for Economic Justice (IEJ) has opened applications for the highly impactful Researcher / Senior Researcher: Global Economic Governance position in Johannesburg, South Africa. This exciting opportunity is designed for experienced researchers and policy professionals who are passionate about economic justice, international financial reform, and progressive transformation across Africa and the world. The position offers successful candidates the chance to work directly on some of the world’s most pressing economic governance issues, including sovereign debt, international taxation, financial regulation, payment systems, and global economic reform. Professionals selected for this role will engage with influential institutions and policy processes such as the G20, African Union (AU), BRICS, governments, civil society organizations, and global financial institutions. This full-time role is offered under a three-year renewable contract and provides competitive salary packages for both Researcher and Senior Researcher levels. About the Institute for Economic Justice (IEJ) The Institute for Economic Justice (IEJ) is a South African policy and research organization focused on advancing economic justice, social equity, and progressive policy reform. Through research, advocacy, and stakeholder engagement, the organization works to transform economic systems in South Africa, across Africa, and internationally. The IEJ collaborates with governments, labour organizations, civil society groups, and international institutions to advocate for fair and sustainable economic systems that support inclusive development. The Global Economic Governance (GEG) programme specifically focuses on reforming the international financial architecture to promote equitable development outcomes. Opportunity Overview Position Title Researcher / Senior Researcher: Global Economic Governance Organization Institute for Economic Justice (IEJ) Location Johannesburg, South Africa Employment Type Full-time Contract Duration Three-year fixed-term contract (renewable depending on funding) Application Deadline 22 May 2026 Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning shortlisted candidates may be contacted before the official closing date. Salary Structure The position offers highly competitive salary packages depending on the level of appointment. Senior Researcher Salary R916,321 – R1,145,401 annually Researcher Salary R687,241 – R859,051 annually These salary ranges reflect the high-level expertise expected from applicants and the strategic importance of the role within the organization. About the Global Economic Governance (GEG) Project The Global Economic Governance Project aims to reform the international financial architecture to support equitable and sustainable economic development globally. The project works on critical issues such as: Sovereign debt reform International taxation Financial regulation Reform of international financial institutions Global payment systems Progressive economic alternatives The project engages with major global and regional policy platforms including: The G20 The African Union (AU) BRICS Governments and policymakers Labour organizations Civil society networks Multilateral institutions This role places successful candidates at the center of global policy debates and economic transformation efforts. Key Responsibilities of the Position The selected candidate will work across research, policy development, advocacy, stakeholder engagement, and project management. 1. Research Responsibilities Successful candidates will: Lead and produce high-quality policy-relevant research Develop publications and analytical reports Coordinate research outputs across IEJ teams Commission and supervise consultant research Prepare policy briefs and accessible summaries Translate technical economic analysis into practical policy recommendations 2. Advocacy Responsibilities The role includes significant advocacy engagement such as: Supporting advocacy strategies with government and civil society partners Developing evidence-based policy proposals Participating in public policy debates Engaging with media platforms including television and radio Producing advocacy and communication materials 3. Strategic Contributions Candidates will also: Contribute to the strategic direction of the GEG programme Participate in high-level policy forums Support IEJ’s wider organizational priorities Contribute to policy discussions at national, regional, and global levels 4. Mentorship and Capacity Building The position includes leadership and mentorship functions: Mentoring junior researchers Reviewing and improving research outputs Supporting internal research capacity development Guiding consultants and junior staff 5. Project Management and Donor Relations The successful candidate will help: Plan and implement projects Prepare and manage budgets Draft donor proposals and technical reports Support project monitoring and reporting activities Required Skills and Experience To qualify for the position, applicants must demonstrate strong academic, analytical, and policy engagement skills. Academic Qualifications Applicants should possess: A PhD for the Senior Researcher position OR A Master’s degree for the Researcher position Relevant fields include: Economics Development Studies Finance Public Administration Sociology Related disciplines Technical Expertise Required The IEJ is seeking candidates with expertise in: International tax systems Sovereign debt governance International financial institutions Payment systems Global financial governance Economic policy analysis Essential Professional Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Strong analytical and writing abilities Ability to communicate technical ideas clearly Experience in policy engagement and advocacy Stakeholder management and networking skills Organizational and project coordination abilities Ability to work independently and collaboratively Experience managing competing priorities under tight deadlines Software and Technical Competencies Candidates should be proficient in: Microsoft Office Suite Statistical software such as: Stata R What IEJ is Looking For in Candidates According to the organization, ideal candidates are: Passionate about economic justice Comfortable working in contested policy environments Skilled at combining research with advocacy Able to engage effectively with policymakers and stakeholders Comfortable in fast-paced policy environments Interested in structural economic reform Committed to progressive transformation in South Africa and Africa Work Environment and Flexibility The role is based in Johannesburg, South Africa, but the organization offers: Flexibility for remote work Opportunities for travel Exposure to international policy discussions and networks Shortlisted applicants may also be asked to: Complete a written assessment Submit writing samples Diversity and Equity Commitment The Institute for Economic Justice has stated its commitment to: Gender equity Racial equity Organizational transformation These considerations will form part of the recruitment and selection process. Why This Opportunity Matters This role is ideal for professionals who want to: Influence global economic policy Work on international financial reform Engage with institutions such as BRICS and the G20 Contribute to African economic transformation Shape real-world policy outcomes Build expertise in international financial governance The position combines academic research with practical advocacy, making it particularly valuable for candidates seeking meaningful policy impact. How to Apply Applicants are required to complete the official application form provided by the Institute for Economic Justice. Applications sent by email will not be accepted. Official Application and Information Links Applicants are encouraged to apply early because the organization reviews applications on a rolling basis. Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post IEJ Senior Researcher: Global Economic Governance Job in South Africa 2026 | Fully Detailed Guide on Salary, Eligibility, Responsibilities, and How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 1325 Scholarship in Memory of Zaida Catalán 2026: Fully Funded One-Year Peacebuilding Placement in Palestine
summary: Applications are now open for the 1325 Scholarship in Memory of Zaida Catalán 2026 , a unique opportunity for early-career Palestinian peacebuilders committed to advancing the Women, Peace and Security (WPS) agenda. Offered by the Folke Bernadotte Academy (FBA) , the scholarship provides a one-year placement with MIFTAH – The Palestinian Initiative for the Promotion of Global Dialogue and Democracy in Ramallah , along with professional capacity-building activities and a basic living allowance. Named in memory of Zaida Catalán, a dedicated peace and human rights advocate, the scholarship aims to strengthen local leadership in gender equality, peacebuilding, and conflict prevention. About the 1325 Scholarship The scholarship is inspired by United Nations Security Council Resolution 1325 on Women, Peace and Security, which emphasizes the critical role of women in conflict prevention, peace negotiations, and post-conflict reconstruction. Through practical experience and structured learning, the program supports young professionals who are committed to advancing women’s rights and peacebuilding in Palestine. Scholarship Benefits Selected scholars will receive: A one-year professional placement with MIFTAH in Ramallah A basic living allowance Tailored capacity-building and professional development activities Mentorship and networking opportunities Hands-on experience in peacebuilding and gender advocacy Host Organization: MIFTAH MIFTAH is a leading Palestinian civil society organization dedicated to promoting democracy, human rights, and global dialogue, with a strong focus on women’s participation and gender justice. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be young peacebuilders in Palestine Have at least two years of relevant work experience in civil society Demonstrate a strong commitment to: Women, Peace and Security Gender equality Peacebuilding Intend to continue working on these issues locally after the scholarship ends Ideal Candidates This scholarship is well suited to early-career professionals working in: Human rights Gender equality Peacebuilding Civil society advocacy Democracy and governance Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 31 May 2026 How to Apply Learn more and submit your application here. For more Scholarship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post 1325 Scholarship in Memory of Zaida Catalán 2026: Fully Funded One-Year Peacebuilding Placement in Palestine appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Vital Voices VV Engage Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women in Public Leadership and Policy
summary: The Vital Voices Global Partnership has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious VV Engage Fellowship 2026, a fully funded 10-month global leadership programme designed to strengthen the capacity, influence, and effectiveness of women leaders shaping public policy and governance across the world. Organized in partnership with Freeport-McMoRan , the VV Engage Fellowship supports outstanding women public leaders who are actively driving policy reform, democratic participation, gender equality, and sustainable development in their communities and countries. The programme seeks to advance women’s public leadership globally while accelerating progress toward the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly: SDG 5: Gender Equality Through world-class leadership training, networking opportunities, mentorship, and access to a powerful global community of women leaders, the fellowship empowers participants to strengthen their leadership impact and drive meaningful social and political change. About the VV Engage Fellowship The VV Engage Fellowship is one of Vital Voices’ signature leadership programmes focused on supporting women in public leadership and policy spaces. The fellowship is designed to: Strengthen women’s political and civic leadership Promote bipartisan collaboration Increase women’s representation in public life Advance gender equality Foster inclusive governance Build leadership capacity among women changemakers The programme brings together women leaders from around the world working across: Government Public policy Civil society Media Community organizing Political movements Main Objectives of the Fellowship The VV Engage Fellowship aims to: Shift global culture around women’s public leadership Increase women’s participation in governance and policy Strengthen leadership effectiveness Promote democratic engagement Support women-led policy reform Advance progress toward the SDGs The fellowship also seeks to create long-term networks of women leaders capable of collaborating across political ideologies and geographic regions. Fellowship Duration The VV Engage Fellowship runs for: 10 months Programme period: June 2026 to March 2027 Participants are expected to actively engage throughout the full fellowship duration. Fellowship Training and Learning Experience The programme includes intensive leadership development and training opportunities. Fellows Will Participate In: 1. Leadership Training Participants will receive advanced training in: Public leadership Governance Strategic communications Political strategy Community mobilization 2. Harvard Kennedy School Collaboration Training will include engagement with: Professors from the Harvard Kennedy School (HKS) This provides fellows with exposure to globally respected expertise in leadership, governance, and policy. 3. Council of Women World Leaders (CWWL) Participants will also engage with: Current and former heads of state Senior political leaders Influential women policymakers through the Council of Women World Leaders network. What Fellows Will Gain The fellowship offers a wide range of personal, professional, and leadership benefits. 1. Public Leadership Skills Fellows will strengthen: Political strategy skills Governance knowledge Strategic communication abilities Public engagement techniques Leadership effectiveness 2. Vital Voices Leadership Journey Participants will complete: The Vital Voices Leadership Journey This virtual leadership development course is based on over 25 years of experience supporting women leaders globally. 3. Global Networking Opportunities Fellows gain access to: A global network of more than 49,000 women leaders International peers and collaborators Policymakers and global influencers 4. Alumni Community Access Participants join: The VV Engage Alumni Community This includes access to: Ongoing virtual programming Alumni engagement opportunities Continued leadership support 5. International Exposure The fellowship connects participants to: Global policy conversations International governance discussions Cross-cultural leadership experiences Who Should Apply? The fellowship is ideal for: Women in public office Women policymakers Political activists Civic leaders Journalists and media professionals Women leading social movements Community organizers Women advocating for governance reform Gender equality advocates Eligible Leadership Categories Applicants must fall under at least one of the following categories: Elected or appointed official Political party leader Political activist Community leader Civil society leader Media professional Eligibility Requirements Applicants must satisfy all programme requirements. 1. Leadership Experience Applicants must have: At least 3 years of leadership or decision-making experience Relevant sectors include: Government Public office Public policy Civic institutions Political movements Civil society organizations 2. Age Requirement Applicants must: Be at least 18 years old by June 2026 3. Commitment to Core Leadership Values Applicants must demonstrate commitment to: Innovation Collaboration Community engagement Gender equality Transparency Good governance 4. Commitment to the Sustainable Development Goals Applicants should demonstrate active commitment to: Advancing the SDGs Social impact leadership Inclusive development Why This Fellowship Matters Women remain underrepresented in: Political leadership Public decision-making Governance structures Policy development The VV Engage Fellowship directly addresses these gaps by: Equipping women leaders with advanced skills Expanding women’s access to leadership networks Supporting women-led governance reform Promoting inclusive public leadership The fellowship also encourages collaboration across political and ideological divides, helping leaders build more inclusive and effective governance systems. Why the Fellowship Stands Out The programme is unique because it combines: Fully funded leadership development Access to Harvard Kennedy School expertise Engagement with global women heads of state International networking opportunities Long-term alumni support SDG-focused leadership training Fellowship Expectations Accepted fellows are expected to: Fully participate throughout the programme Engage actively with trainers and fellows Complete programme activities Contribute meaningfully to fellowship discussions and collaboration Selection Process The VV Engage Fellowship is highly competitive. Shortlisted Applicants May Be Invited To: A 30-minute virtual interview Final selection decisions will be communicated following the review and interview process. Application Deadline Applications for the VV Engage Fellowship 2026 must be submitted by: 22 May 2026 at 11:59 PM EST Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the highly competitive nature of the programme. Please click HERE to submit your application! About Vital Voices Global Partnership Vital Voices is a globally recognized organization focused on: Women’s leadership Gender equality Social impact Public leadership development Global collaboration The organization has supported thousands of women leaders worldwide through: Fellowships Leadership training Mentorship Advocacy initiatives Global networking opportunities Official Contact Information For additional information about the fellowship, applicants may contact: VVEngage@VitalVoices.org For more information about the Vital Voices VV Engage Fellowship 2026, click HERE . Visit HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize 2026: Win US$100,000 for Promoting Tolerance and Non-Violence Worldwide
summary: Nominations are now open for the 2026 UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize for the Promotion of Tolerance and Non-Violence , one of the world’s most prestigious international awards recognizing outstanding efforts to advance peace, dialogue, and inclusion. Presented by UNESCO , the prize is awarded every two years and carries a US$100,000 award . It honours individuals, institutions, entities, and non-governmental organizations that have made exceptional contributions to promoting tolerance and non-violence through science, art, culture, and communication. The award celebrates initiatives that foster mutual understanding, respect for diversity, and peaceful coexistence across communities and nations. About the UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize Established in 1995 on the initiative and generosity of UNESCO Goodwill Ambassador Madanjeet Singh, the prize recognizes achievements that align with UNESCO’s commitment to building peace through education, culture, science, and communication. The award seeks to highlight innovative and impactful efforts that address intolerance, discrimination, and violence while promoting dialogue and human dignity. Prize Award The laureate will receive: US$100,000 cash prize International recognition from UNESCO Global visibility for their work and impact Who Can Be Nominated Eligible nominees include: Individuals Institutions Organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Nominees must demonstrate significant leadership and measurable impact in promoting: Tolerance Non-violence Peacebuilding Dialogue Inclusion Respect for diversity Areas of Contribution Nominations are encouraged for work carried out in: Science Arts Culture Communication Education Community development Why This Prize Matters The UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize highlights the importance of grassroots and institutional efforts to counter hatred, discrimination, and violence. It recognizes those whose work helps create more peaceful, just, and inclusive societies worldwide. Nomination Deadline All nominations must be submitted by: 19 June 2026 For additional information, contact: tolerance.prize@unesco.org How to Submit a Nomination Learn more about the eligibility criteria and nomination process here. For more Award Opportunities, Visit OFY here. title: Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 in Canada: Funding for Post-Secondary Students Honouring the Victims of Flight PS752
summary: Applications are now open for the Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 , a meaningful scholarship initiative that honours the lives and legacies of the victims of Ukraine International Airlines Flight PS752. The program supports full-time post-secondary students studying in Canada whose academic work aligns with the educational and professional backgrounds of the victims or focuses on air disaster prevention and aviation safety. In 2025, the scholarship program awarded 36 scholarships to Canadian and international students from 19 educational institutions across six provinces , including doctoral, master’s, bachelor’s, and diploma students. This scholarship provides both financial support and an opportunity for students to contribute to fields of study that reflect the aspirations and accomplishments of those lost in the tragedy. About the Scholarship Program The Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program was established to preserve the memory of the victims by supporting students whose studies embody similar areas of academic and professional excellence. Eligible applicants must demonstrate how their current field of study connects to: The academic or professional backgrounds of the victims of Flight PS752, or Research and initiatives related to air disaster prevention and aviation safety Scholarship Benefits Selected recipients receive financial assistance to support their post-secondary education in Canada. In addition to funding, recipients become part of an important legacy initiative that recognizes academic excellence, innovation, and remembrance. Who Can Apply Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be enrolled as a full-time student Study at a Canadian post-secondary institution , including universities and community colleges Have completed at least one year of their current program Have at least one year remaining in their studies Demonstrate alignment between their field of study and the scholarship’s commemorative objectives Eligible Institutions Students from all recognized post-secondary institutions in Canada are eligible, including: Universities Community colleges Polytechnic institutions Both Canadian and international students studying in Canada may apply. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 9 June 2026 How to Apply Learn more and apply here. For more Scholarship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 in Canada: Funding for Post-Secondary Students Honouring the Victims of Flight PS752 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
